steps.title: Rewrite the radical
steps.content:
Use the absolute value identity $\sqrt{a^2}=|a|$.
$$\sqrt{(x+15)^2}=|x+15|$$
So the equation becomes
$$|x+15|=x+15$$

steps.title: Find when it is true
steps.content:
An absolute value equals the original expression when that expression is nonnegative.
$$x+15\geq 0$$
$$x\geq -15$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph or solve the equation directly. The solution set begins at its least value, so match that least value to the choices. The answer is B.
